commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds two-tier pricing metadata for OpenCode Go’s `qwen3.7-plus` model and adds catalog-resolution and high-context cost-estimator coverage.

PR surface: Source +16, Tests +42. Total +58 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. With this PR’s ranges and the current half-open selector, exactly 256,000 input tokens deterministically select the high tier despite the official `≤256K` low-tier contract.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is a narrow mechanical range-boundary and regression-test repair on the PR branch.

target.title: fix(opencode-go): add qwen plus tiered pricing
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds the official `qwen3.7-plus` tiered pricing metadata to the bundled `opencode-go` catalog.
- Keeps the existing flat first-tier prices as the fallback/default row values, while adding the `>256K` tier used by cost estimation paths that understand `tieredPricing`.
- Adds focused coverage for the opencode-go catalog row and the high-context cost estimator behavior.
- Intentionally does not change provider routing, live catalog discovery, auth, or inference transport behavior.

## Linked context

Closes #91238

Related #91239

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- Not directly. This follows the live issue and the ClawSweeper review on #91239, which identified missing `qwen3.7-plus` tiered pricing as the remaining merge risk.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `opencode-go/qwen3.7-plus` no longer underestimates high-context usage-cost summaries by using only the `<=256K` flat pricing row while advertising a 1M context window.
- Real environment tested: macOS arm64 worktree from `upstream/main` at `538d36eaaa`, Node v24.15.0, pnpm v11.2.2, local OpenClaw source checkout.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/zen/go/v1/models | node -e 'let s="";process.stdin.on("data",d=>s+=d);process.stdin.on("end",()=>{const j=JSON.parse(s); const ids=(j.data||j.models||[]).map(m=>m.id||m.name).filter(id=>["minimax-m3","qwen3.7-max","qwen3.7-plus"].includes(id)); console.log(ids.join("\\n"));})'
node --import tsx - <<'EOF_NODE'
import { registerSingleProviderPlugin } from 'openclaw/plugin-sdk/plugin-test-runtime';
import { estimateUsageCost } from './src/utils/usage-format.ts';
import plugin from './extensions/opencode-go/index.ts';
const provider = await registerSingleProviderPlugin(plugin);
const model = provider.resolveDynamicModel?.({ modelId: 'qwen3.7-plus' });
const usage = { input: 300_000, output: 1_000, cacheRead: 10_000, cacheWrite: 2_000 };
console.log(JSON.stringify({
  model: model?.provider + '/' + model?.id,
  contextWindow: model?.contextWindow,
  flatCost: model?.cost && estimateUsageCost({ usage, cost: { input: model.cost.input, output: model.cost.output, cacheRead: model.cost.cacheRead, cacheWrite: model.cost.cacheWrite } }),
  tieredCost: estimateUsageCost({ usage, cost: model?.cost }),
  tieredPricing: model?.cost.tieredPricing,
}, null, 2));
EOF_NODE
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/opencode-go/index.test.ts src/utils/usage-format.test.ts
pnpm test:extension opencode-go
git diff --check
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
minimax-m3
qwen3.7-max
qwen3.7-plus

{
  "model": "opencode-go/qwen3.7-plus",
  "contextWindow": 1000000,
  "flatCost": 0.123,
  "tieredCost": 0.369,
  "tieredPricing": [
    { "input": 0.4, "output": 1.6, "cacheRead": 0.04, "cacheWrite": 0.5, "range": [0, 256000] },
    { "input": 1.2, "output": 4.8, "cacheRead": 0.12, "cacheWrite": 1.5, "range": [256000] }
  ]
}

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/opencode-go/index.test.ts src/utils/usage-format.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1); Tests 33 passed (33)
Test Files  1 passed (1); Tests 11 passed (11)
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards

pnpm test:extension opencode-go
Test Files  4 passed (4); Tests 15 passed (15)

git diff --check
(no output)
```

- Observed result after fix: the opencode-go runtime catalog resolves `qwen3.7-plus` with the official two-tier pricing metadata, and `estimateUsageCost` selects the high-context tier for a 300K-input usage sample, producing `$0.369` instead of the flat first-tier `$0.123`.
- What was not tested: authenticated live inference through OpenCode Go and a real gateway `models list` run. The local gateway on this machine required pairing/scope approval, so I did not use it as proof.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this proof validates the provider catalog/runtime registration path and usage-cost estimator behavior from source. It does not claim to change transport-time flat cost calculation; session usage aggregation already recomputes tiered pricing when configured.
- Before evidence: current `upstream/main` already includes the new model IDs, but `qwen3.7-plus` only carried the flat `<=256K` rates, so the same 300K-input sample would estimate `$0.123` from flat metadata instead of `$0.369` from the official high-context tier.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/opencode-go/index.test.ts src/utils/usage-format.test.ts`
- `pnpm test:extension opencode-go`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --write extensions/opencode-go/provider-catalog.ts extensions/opencode-go/index.test.ts src/utils/usage-format.test.ts`
- `pnpm lint:extensions:no-src-outside-plugin-sdk`
- `git diff --check`
- `codex review --base upstream/main` attempted twice; it timed out after 120s and 300s before returning a final verdict.

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `extensions/opencode-go/index.test.ts` now asserts the concrete `qwen3.7-plus` tiered catalog metadata.
- `src/utils/usage-format.test.ts` now covers the official `qwen3.7-plus` high-context tier with a >256K input sample.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- #91239's ClawSweeper review flagged that a 1M-context `qwen3.7-plus` row with only `<=256K` flat pricing would understate high-context usage costs.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Usage-cost summaries that consume catalog `tieredPricing` can now report the official high-context `qwen3.7-plus` rate.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Pricing metadata accuracy for `qwen3.7-plus`.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The values match the current official OpenCode Go docs and are covered by catalog and estimator tests.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Waiting for ClawSweeper and maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- Maintainer review and CI/ClawSweeper.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Addresses the ClawSweeper finding on #91239: represent official `qwen3.7-plus` `<=256K` and `>256K` pricing tiers and add focused tests.

AI-assisted: yes. I understand the code change and ran the validation above.

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
